gauge.name: Fox River at McHenry (Stratton Lock and Dam) tailwater

impacts.statement: McHenry Waste Water Treatment Plant property is threatened. Miller Road is threatened west of the river in McHenry.
Multiple structures along the river are threatened in downtown McHenry. River Road is threatened at IL-120 (Elm Street) in McHenry.
IL-176 is threatened near Holiday Hills.
Country Club Drive, Emerald Drive, and River Road near Chapel Hill Road are threatened in McHenry.
Residences threatened along Country Club Drive and Emerald Drive in McHenry. Roadways threatened at Miller Riverfront Park in McHenry.
Residences along River Road are threatened near Mary Lane in McHenry. Backwater causes Boone Creek to threaten parking lots in downtown McHenry. Numerous marinas are threatened in McHenry.
Low-lying sections of Miller Riverfront Park inundated in McHenry.
Backwater causes Boone Creek to inundate sections of the McHenry river walk.
Water reaches bankfull along Country Club Drive and Emerald Drive in McHenry.
Roadways and structures may be threatened in Frisch's Riverside subdivision along Lagoon and River Shore Drives.
Roadways and structures may be threatened in the Holiday Hills subdivison along Holiday Drive.
Boathouses threatened along the river downstream of the dam.
Fox Waterway Agency considers "no wake" order for the Fox River from Algonquin Dam to Stratton Dam.

dataAttribution.text: USGS--Water Resources of the United States
dataAttribution.title: US Geological Survey
Observations courtesy of U.S. Geological Survey

impactsLowWaters.impact: Low water levels begin to impact operations at Stratton and Algonquin Dams.
Approximate stage of the 1-in-10 annual chance flow (7-day average).

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are not available. Only observed stages are available for this point.
